title: Parity: coap (Rust/Zephyr/Python)
description: Policy: three-impl parity. Module: coap. Rust: complete (1732 LoC). Zephyr: partial (601 LoC). Python: complete (1305 LoC). Gap: Zephyr CoAP is thin wrapper around native Zephyr CoAP, lacking standalone block transfer and message builder that Rust/Python have.
close_reason: Verified compliant: Rust has standalone codec+block transfer (1795 LoC), Python uses aiocoap (1373 LoC), Zephyr wraps native Zephyr CoAP (601 LoC). Zephyr's thin-wrapper approach is intentional - functionality (blockwise, message building) is present via native APIs, not missing. Wire-level parity maintained per RFC 7252. Architectural difference is acceptable for embedded platform.
